skill.name: mcp-apps-builder
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through building, modifying, debugging, and reviewing mcp-use MCP servers with tools, resources, prompts, widgets, authentication, deployment, and response helpers.
skill.summary: Build and review mcp-use MCP servers with structured implementation guidance.
skill.author: mcp-use

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but contextual review found them in Markdown documentation, examples, relative links, and license text rather than executable skill code. No confirmed malicious intent, credential exfiltration, prompt-injection attempt, or runnable hidden behavior was found. The main residual risk is that users may copy example commands, network calls, environment variable handling, or development-only options without adapting them safely.

low_findings.title: Static external command matches are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The numerous external command findings are caused by fenced Markdown examples and CLI guidance. They document commands such as scaffolding, running development servers, and deployment checks, but the skill package does not include executable scripts that run them automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation code fences and prose. No executable script file or automatic command runner was present in the file inventory.

low_findings.title: Network findings are example URLs and documented API calls
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs and fetch examples appear in authentication and tool documentation. They show OAuth discovery, provider APIs, localhost callbacks, and example weather APIs, not hidden outbound requests made by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network indicators are inside Markdown examples. They are visible implementation guidance and do not execute during skill loading.

low_findings.title: Credential and environment findings are secret-handling guidance
low_findings.description: Environment variable and secret-key matches are present because the references teach users to configure OAuth providers, API keys, and deployment environment variables. This is legitimate for an MCP server builder, though users must avoid copying placeholder secrets into production.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are tables, examples, and explicit guidance to avoid committing secrets. The usage is relevant to authentication documentation.

low_findings.title: Filesystem path traversal matches are relative documentation links
low_findings.description: Path traversal findings point to Markdown relative links such as ../server/tools.md and ../../SKILL.md. These are documentation navigation links, not file reads using user-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched strings are Markdown links in next-step sections. There is no code path performing filesystem access at these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto and exec matches are scanner token collisions
low_findings.description: Weak cryptographic algorithm findings include ordinary words in the Apache license and authentication documentation, while the Python exec finding is actually a SQLite db.exec schema example. These are not evidence of unsafe cryptography or dynamic code execution in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled matches are legal text, headings, and a database DDL helper call. None is a cryptographic implementation or Python exec call.

low_findings.title: Forceful skill-routing language is instructional but not an override attempt
low_findings.description: The skill uses mandatory language telling assistants to read relevant mcp-use references before MCP work. This can influence assistant behavior, but it does not ask the evaluator to ignore security rules, skip review, claim pre-approval, or change risk levels.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording is strong but limited to skill usage and navigation. No prompt-injection text matching the audit warning categories was found in the reviewed context.

content.value_statement: MCP server work often fails when tools, resources, prompts, widgets, and authentication are mixed without clear patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused mcp-use guidance for implementation, review, and deployment.
actual_capabilities: Routes MCP development tasks to the correct mcp-use reference file.
Explains when to use tools, resources, prompts, and widgets.
Provides patterns for WorkOS, Supabase, and custom OAuth setup.
Shows response helper usage for text, objects, markdown, widgets, and errors.
Documents widget patterns for state, interactivity, themes, and advanced behavior.
Lists deployment steps for Manufact Cloud and self-hosted MCP servers.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not execute or scaffold projects by itself.
Examples use placeholders and must be adapted to each application.
Authentication guidance depends on provider configuration outside the skill.
It focuses on mcp-use patterns, not every MCP framework.

use_cases.title: Create a new MCP server
use_cases.description: Use the quickstart and architecture references to scaffold a mcp-use server and add the first tool.

use_cases.title: Add authenticated MCP features
use_cases.description: Use the authentication references to choose WorkOS, Supabase, or a custom OAuth provider.

use_cases.title: Review MCP implementation quality
use_cases.description: Use the checklists and common patterns to review validation, response formatting, widgets, and deployment readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Server
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-apps-builder skill to help me create a new mcp-use MCP server with one simple tool.

prompt_templates.title: Add Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-apps-builder authentication references to add Supabase or WorkOS authentication to my MCP server.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Widget Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-apps-builder widget references to design a React widget that shows data and calls a server tool.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an MCP Server
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-apps-builder references to review my MCP server for architecture, validation, response helpers, authentication, and deployment risks.

output_examples.input: Create a new MCP server with a greeting action.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the quickstart and tools references, scaffolds the project, adds a focused greeting tool, and explains how to run it locally.

output_examples.input: Add a visual browser for search results.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a widget-backed workflow, defines which data belongs in tool output, and outlines state handling in the widget.

output_examples.input: Review my MCP server before deployment.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks validation, response helpers, authentication configuration, secret handling, deployment prerequisites, and known mcp-use anti-patterns.

best_practices: Read the specific reference file before changing a matching MCP primitive.
Use Zod schemas and response helpers for predictable tool behavior.
Keep secrets in environment variables and document required configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not manually recreate framework boilerplate when the mcp-use scaffold is appropriate.
Do not expose broad tools when several focused tools would be clearer.
Do not copy placeholder API keys, URLs, or secrets into production code.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build mcp-use MCP servers with tools, resources, prompts, widgets, authentication, and deployment guidance.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill. The assistant may suggest commands, but execution depends on the active tool environment.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it help with OAuth?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes references for WorkOS, Supabase, and custom identity providers.

faq.question: Can it create visual MCP widgets?
faq.answer: It provides widget guidance for React components, state, interactivity, themes, and advanced UI patterns.

faq.question: What should users verify before production?
faq.answer: Users should verify input validation, authentication, secret storage, error handling, rate limits, and deployment configuration.
